reasoning: In Germany, the only entity with the legal authority to ban a political party is the Federal Constitutional Court (Bundesverfassungsgericht), protected by tight constitutional rules (the Parteienprivileg) that prevent the government from simply banning a party via executive order. While a group of lawmakers introduced a motion in late January 2025 to ask the Bundestag to file a ban application against the AfD, the process of investigating, hearing, and delivering a verdict on a party ban historically takes several years. The 2013-2017 attempt to ban the NPD took nearly four years; the 1956 KPD ban took five years. Given that it is already May 2025 and an application has yet to even advance to court action, it is legally and practically impossible for the BVerfG to conclude proceedings and officially announce a ban by July 1, 2025. Bounding my answer to the 0.05 limit as strictly imposed by the prompt rules.
